evals.prompt: Check our NestJS + React SaaS app for GDPR compliance. We collect user email, name, address, and payment info. We use Stripe for payments and SendGrid for emails. Users are in the EU.
evals.expected_output: GDPR compliance report with PII inventory, consent mechanisms check, data flow tracing, right to deletion verification

assertions.name: pii_inventory
assertions.description: Lists all PII fields found (email, name, address, payment)

assertions.name: consent_check
assertions.description: Checks for cookie consent, data processing consent, marketing opt-in

assertions.name: right_to_deletion
assertions.description: Verifies data deletion mechanism exists for GDPR Article 17

assertions.name: data_flow_traced
assertions.description: Traces PII from collection through Stripe/SendGrid to storage

assertions.name: findings_with_evidence
assertions.description: Each finding cites specific file:line

evals.prompt: Verify PCI-DSS compliance for our payment integration. We use Stripe Elements on the frontend (card details never touch our server) and Stripe API on the backend for charges. Check if we're handling payment data correctly.
evals.expected_output: PCI-DSS compliance check focused on cardholder data handling, Stripe integration patterns, and logging

assertions.name: card_data_handling
assertions.description: Verifies card numbers never touch our server (Stripe Elements handles PCI)

assertions.name: stripe_key_security
assertions.description: Checks that Stripe secret key is in env vars, not hardcoded

assertions.name: logging_check
assertions.description: Verifies payment logs don't contain card numbers or CVV

assertions.name: https_enforcement
assertions.description: Verifies all payment-related endpoints use HTTPS

assertions.name: scope_assessment
assertions.description: Determines PCI-DSS scope (SAQ-A for Stripe Elements vs SAQ-D)

evals.prompt: Check HIPAA compliance for our healthcare appointment booking app. We store patient names, dates of birth, appointment history, and medical notes. Backend is Django, database is PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: HIPAA compliance report covering PHI handling, encryption, access controls, and audit logging

assertions.name: phi_inventory
assertions.description: Lists all Protected Health Information fields found

assertions.name: encryption_at_rest
assertions.description: Checks if PHI is encrypted in the database

assertions.name: encryption_in_transit
assertions.description: Verifies HTTPS/TLS for all PHI transmission

assertions.name: access_controls
assertions.description: Reviews role-based access to PHI data

assertions.name: audit_logging
assertions.description: Checks for audit trail on PHI access and modifications

evals.prompt: Run a CCPA compliance check on our e-commerce platform. We're a California-based business selling to US consumers. We collect email, browsing history, purchase history, and IP addresses. We share data with our analytics provider (Mixpanel) and ad platform (Google Ads).
evals.expected_output: CCPA compliance report covering data collection notice, opt-out rights, third-party sharing, and data deletion

assertions.name: data_collection_notice
assertions.description: Checks for privacy policy/notice at point of data collection

assertions.name: opt_out_mechanism
assertions.description: Verifies 'Do Not Sell My Data' mechanism exists

assertions.name: third_party_sharing
assertions.description: Reviews data shared with Mixpanel and Google Ads for CCPA compliance

assertions.name: data_deletion
assertions.description: Checks for consumer data deletion capability

assertions.name: data_inventory
assertions.description: All collected data types identified (email, browsing, purchase, IP)

evals.prompt: Check SOC 2 Type II readiness for our B2B SaaS platform. Focus on the Security and Availability trust service criteria. We run on AWS ECS, use PostgreSQL on RDS, and have a team of 10 developers.
evals.expected_output: SOC 2 readiness assessment covering security controls, availability, access management, and change management

assertions.name: security_controls
assertions.description: Reviews encryption, network security, vulnerability management

assertions.name: access_management
assertions.description: Checks MFA, least privilege, access reviews

assertions.name: availability_controls
assertions.description: Reviews uptime targets, redundancy, disaster recovery

assertions.name: change_management
assertions.description: Checks for code review process, CI/CD, and deployment controls

assertions.name: findings_with_evidence
assertions.description: Each finding cites specific evidence or gap
